pattern: Bounded 0/1 knapsack: instead of just tracking which sums are reachable, track the longest subsequence length that reaches each sum.
hints: This looks like a subset-sum reachability question, but it's asking for something stronger than yes/no — think about what extra information you'd need to track per achievable sum.
Treat it as a 0/1 knapsack over sums 0..target: for each element, decide once whether to include it, and keep the best (longest) subsequence length that lands on each sum.
Maintain dp[s] = the longest subsequence length summing to s (or -infinity if unreachable), and update it by scanning sums from high to low for each number so no element is reused.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates give multiple equally short ways to reach the sum
one big element hits the target directly, but many small ones give a strictly longer subsequence
target equals the sum of the whole array, so every element must be used
single element at the maximum allowed value, matching the maximum target

stress.description: large arrays with target values biased toward reachable sums via a constructed subset, mixed with fully random targets
